name: New York Tuition Assistance Program (TAP)
administered_by: New York State Higher Education Services Corporation (HESC)
description: New York's primary state grant program providing need-based tuition assistance to eligible residents attending in-state colleges. One of the largest state grant programs in the nation.
eligibility: New York State resident, U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen, enrolled full-time in an approved NYS degree program, meeting income thresholds. Must be in-state student for at least 12 months before applying.
award_amount: Varies from $500 to $5,665 per year based on NYS taxable income, family size, and type of institution (public vs private). Full-time students from families with incomes up to $80,000 may qualify.
application_process: Apply via FAFSA by May 1 deadline. NYS processes ISIR data and sends eligibility notice. Students must confirm college enrollment to receive award. Separate TAP application is no longer required if using FAFSA.
timeline: May 1 priority deadline. Awards processed in June-July. Must be renewed annually with satisfactory academic progress (minimum 12 credits per semester toward degree completion).
advisor_notes: TAP is tied to tuition only — it does not cover fees, room, or board. Undergraduate students can receive TAP for up to 8 semesters (4 years full-time or 6 years part-time). Income thresholds are generous compared to other states. Part-time students may receive reduced TAP.
stacking_notes: TAP generally stacks with Pell Grant. However, the combination of TAP and other tuition-specific aid cannot exceed tuition and fee costs. Some colleges reduce institutional grants when TAP is received — review school policy. TAP does not typically affect SEOG or Work-Study eligibility.
